tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I need to continue my research on cryptographic solutions and their relationship to socio-economic inequality. Before I add anything new, can you show me what I already have stored about this topic? I want to make sure I'm not duplicating work or missing context from previous sessions.

Based on my search, your memory storage is currently empty — there are no stored memories about cryptographic solutions, socio-economic inequality, or related research topics.

This means you're starting fresh, so there's no prior context to duplicate. Would you like to add your existing research notes or findings now so they're saved for future sessions?

Yes, I'd like to add my research findings now. I've been looking into how cryptographic solutions—like zero-knowledge proofs and decentralized identity systems—might address certain dimensions of socio-economic inequality. My notes include a few key areas: how privacy-preserving credentials could improve access to financial services for underbanked populations, case studies on blockchain-based land registry systems reducing property disputes in developing regions, and some critical perspectives on whether these technologies actually reach marginalized communities or just create new forms of digital exclusion. Can you save these findings so I can build on them in future sessions?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Research on cryptographic solutions and socio-economic inequality. Key areas of focus: (1) Privacy-preserving credentials (e.g., zero-knowledge proofs) and their potential to improve access to financial services for underbanked populations; (2) Case studies on blockchain-based land registry systems reducing property disputes in developing regions; (3) Critical perspectives on whether cryptographic/decentralized technologies actually reach marginalized communities or create new forms of digital exclusion."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Research on cryptographic solutions and socio-economic inequality. Key areas of focus: (1) Privacy-preserving credentials (e.g., zero-knowledge proofs) and their potential to improve access to financial services for underbanked populations; (2) Case studies on blockchain-based land registry systems reducing property disputes in developing regions; (3) Critical perspectives on whether cryptographic/decentralized technologies actually reach marginalized communities or create new forms of digital exclusion."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Your research findings have been saved. The memory covers your three key areas:

1. **Privacy-preserving credentials** — zero-knowledge proofs for financial inclusion
2. **Blockchain land registries** — case studies from developing regions
3. **Critical perspectives** — digital exclusion risks for marginalized communities

You're all set to continue building on this in future sessions. Let me know when you'd like to add more findings or explore a specific direction.

Now that I've added my research findings, can you show me what's currently stored so I can verify the context is complete and accurate? I want to make sure all three key areas are properly captured before I move forward.
